title: The Packaging PDK Is the Missing Layer for Co-Packaged Optics - Semiwiki
gist: Moh Kolb argues that co-packaged optics (CPO) will not scale on photonic device performance alone; the missing enabler is a packaging PDK that acts as a realization contract binding optical, electrical, thermal, mechanical, test, and manufacturing domains, elevating CPO from a device-design problem to a system-realization problem for AI infrastructure.

themes.theme: PDK redefinition: from device kit to realization interface

insights.claim: For co-packaged optics, the process design kit must evolve from a device-design kit into a realization interface kit that describes the packaged electro-optical system, not just the photonic device.
insights.speaker: Moh Kolb
insights.so_what: Signals a new EDA/packaging tooling layer whose absence is a bottleneck; vendors who deliver packaging PDKs could capture a strategic control point in the optics supply chain.
insights.quote: For CPO, the PDK must evolve from a device-design kit into a realization interface kit.
insights.locator: From Photonic Device Design to Electro-Optical Realization

insights.claim: A traditional PDK is not merely a file set but a contract between design and manufacturing that defines the design space realizable with confidence.
insights.speaker: Moh Kolb
insights.so_what: Framing the PDK as a contract clarifies why a missing packaging PDK stalls productization even when devices work in the lab.
insights.quote: The PDK is not only a convenience file set. It is a contract between design and manufacturing.
insights.locator: The Traditional Role of the PDK

insights.claim: The author proposes a three-tier PDK hierarchy: the PDK defines what can be drawn, the packaging PDK defines what can be integrated, and electro-optical realization defines what can be built, tested, yielded, calibrated, and trusted.
insights.speaker: Moh Kolb
insights.so_what: A mental model for mapping where new tool and IP investment must go; the highest tier (realization) is the least served today.
insights.quote: PDK defines what can be drawn. Packaging PDK defines what can be integrated. Electro-optical realization defines what can be built, tested, yielded, calibrated, and trusted.

themes.theme: The copper-light boundary as the decisive integration point

insights.claim: CPO is fundamentally about moving the copper-light boundary closer to the compute engine, and that boundary carries its own burden of coupling loss, alignment sensitivity, thermal drift, package stress, calibration, and reliability exposure.
insights.speaker: Moh Kolb
insights.so_what: Reframes CPO evaluation around the boundary rather than the device, redirecting where yield and reliability risk actually concentrate.
insights.quote: It is about moving the copper-light boundary closer to the compute engine. That boundary is not free.

insights.claim: Electrical signals over copper face reach, loss, crosstalk, impedance discontinuity, equalization power, and heat challenges as bandwidth and distance increase, which is the physical driver pushing optics toward the compute engine.
insights.speaker: Moh Kolb
insights.so_what: Explains why copper's limits make CPO adoption a when-not-if for high-bandwidth AI systems; equalization power is a specific cost lever to watch.

insights.claim: A common CPO failure mode is where the device looks good and the package looks good but the system still fails at the interface, which realization-aware packaging PDKs are meant to prevent.
insights.speaker: Moh Kolb
insights.so_what: Interface-level failure is the hidden yield killer in CPO; tooling that de-risks it addresses the true blocker to volume deployment.
insights.quote: A realization-aware packaging PDK should help prevent the common failure mode where the device looks good, the package looks good, and the system still fails at the interface.
insights.locator: From Static Rules to Realization-Aware Environments

themes.theme: Device excellence is necessary but insufficient

insights.claim: A good modulator, laser, PIC, and EIC each still have to survive the package, couple into the optical path, align with fiber attach, and drive through a clean electrical path, so better photonic devices alone will not scale CPO.
insights.speaker: Moh Kolb
insights.so_what: Directs investor attention away from device-performance headlines toward packaging, test, and calibration capability as the real differentiators.
insights.quote: The industry needs better photonic devices. But better photonic devices alone are not enough.

insights.claim: For CPO the package is not passive but becomes the place where optical, electrical, thermal, mechanical, and manufacturing domains converge.
insights.speaker: Moh Kolb
insights.so_what: Elevates advanced packaging vendors and OSATs as the locus of CPO value capture and risk, not just PIC/EIC makers.
insights.quote: For CPO, the package is not passive.
insights.locator: The Packaging PDK as a Realization Contract

themes.theme: AI infrastructure and the data-movement bottleneck

insights.claim: AI infrastructure is increasingly constrained by data movement between accelerators, memory, switches, racks, and clusters, which drives power, latency, thermal load, bandwidth limits, and system cost and is why optical I/O and CPO are receiving heavy attention.
insights.speaker: Moh Kolb
insights.so_what: Data movement, not compute, is the framed constraint, supporting a durable demand thesis for optical interconnect and CPO enablers.
insights.quote: AI infrastructure is increasingly constrained by data movement.
insights.locator: Why This Matters for AI Infrastructure

insights.claim: A data center cannot operate on record-setting lab performance; it needs manufacturable modules, stable coupling, reliable attach, controlled thermal behavior, test coverage, calibration flows, field diagnostics, and supply-chain repeatability.
insights.speaker: Moh Kolb
insights.so_what: Manufacturability and supply-chain repeatability, not peak specs, will determine which CPO suppliers win volume design-ins.
insights.quote: A data center cannot operate on record-setting lab performance.
insights.locator: Why This Matters for AI Infrastructure

insights.claim: The next phase of CPO will be won not by optical performance alone but by teams that can define, integrate, test, calibrate, manufacture, and trust the full electro-optical realization path.
insights.speaker: Moh Kolb
insights.so_what: A competitive-moat thesis: integration and test capability, not device benchmarks, is the differentiator to screen suppliers on.
insights.quote: The next phase of CPO will not be won by optical performance alone.
